km.Devon, England, United Kingdom: ដិវ៉ុន, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស, uណៃteត គីnហ្គដុម

zh.lifeSummary: 當William de la Peyre or Perry於1220年出生在英國英格蘭戴文時，他的父親Roger de la Peyre or Perry22歲，母親Lady Roger de la Peyre17歲。他和Lady William de la Peyre至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de la Peyre or Perry于1220年出生在英国英格兰戴文时，他的父亲Roger de la Peyre or Perry22岁，母亲Lady Roger de la Peyre17岁。他和Lady William de la Peyre至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Peyre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Pear  .

variants.name: Pear
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Dov.
English: topographic name from residence by a pear tree (Middle English pere, Old English peru, pere). Compare Perry .
English: nickname from Middle English pere, Old French per, peer ‘peer or paragon’, also ‘companion’.

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: Perry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Herry ‘son of Herry’, a variant of the personal name Harry (see Harris ).

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a pear tree (Middle English piri(e), peri(e), puri(e), Old English pirige, pyrige), or a habitational name for someone who owned or came from a place so named. This surname and a number of variants have been established in Ireland since the 17th century.

variants.name: Peery
meanings: Irish: variant of   Perry  , taken to Ireland at the time of thePlantation of Ulster and in the Cromwellian period.

variants.name: Pear
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Dov.
English: topographic name from residence by a pear tree (Middle English pere, Old English peru, pere). Compare Perry .
English: nickname from Middle English pere, Old French per, peer ‘peer or paragon’, also ‘companion’.

variants.name: Peary
meanings: English (mainly northern): variant of   Perry  .

History: Robert E. Peary (1856–1920) was a U.S.-born explorer, who in 1909 led the first successful expedition to the North Pole. Peary Land in the north of Greenland is named after him, as he explored there in 1892 and 1900.

variants.name: Perrie
meanings: Scottish: variant of Perry 2. Alternatively, perhaps from the Old French personal name Pierre; see Pierce .

variants.name: Harris
meanings: English (southern England and south Wales): from the personal name Harry + genitival -s. This surname is also established in Ireland, taken there principally during the Plantation of Ulster. However, in some cases, particularly in families coming from County Mayo, Harris can be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEarchadha. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

American shortened and altered form of Greek surnames begining with Cha(r)-, such as Chasandrinos (variant of Kassandrinos, a habitational name from the Kassandra peninsula of Chalkidiki), and various patronymics from the personal name Charalampos (see Charos ). In North America, the surname Harris may possibly also originate from a transferred use of the Greek personal (given) name Charis or Harris (shortened forms of Charalampos) as a surname (i.e. as a replacement of the original surname).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Perryman
meanings: English (Devon): elaborated form of Perry , with Middle English man, probably denoting one who lived near a pear tree; see Pearman .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William de la Peyre or Perry was born in 1220, in Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, Roger de la Peyre or Perry, was 22 and his mother, Lady Roger de la Peyre, was 17. He had at least 1 son with Lady William de la Peyre.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de la Peyre or Perry est né en 1220, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Roger de la Peyre or Perry, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Lady Roger de la Peyre, avait 17 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Lady William de la Peyre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de la Peyre or Perry 1220 in Devon, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger de la Peyre or Perry 22 und seine Mutter Lady Roger de la Peyre 17. Er hatte mit Lady William de la Peyre mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William de la Peyre or Perry è nato il nel 1220 nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Roger de la Peyre or Perry, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Lady Roger de la Peyre, aveva 17 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Lady William de la Peyre.

ja.Devon, England, United Kingdom: イギリスイギリスデボン
ja.lifeSummary: William de la Peyre or Perryが1220年にイギリスイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父Roger de la Peyre or Perryは22歳，母Lady Roger de la Peyreは17歳であった。彼はLady William de la Peyreとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: William de la Peyre or Perry(이)가 1220년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger de la Peyre or Perry(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Roger de la Peyre(은)는 17세였다. 그는 Lady William de la Peyre(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de la Peyre or Perry nasceu em 1220, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Roger de la Peyre or Perry, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Lady Roger de la Peyre, tinha 17 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Lady William de la Peyre.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de la Peyre or Perry родился в 1220 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Roger de la Peyre or Perry было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Roger de la Peyre, 17. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Lady William de la Peyre.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William de la Peyre or Perry nació en 1220, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Roger de la Peyre or Perry, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Lady Roger de la Peyre, tenía 17 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Lady William de la Peyre.
